zh.lifeSummary: 當Hersende de Sille- le- Guillaume於1190年出生在法國薩特希利艾勒-吉耀奧時，她的父親Guillaume III de Sillé le Guillaume 5ème Seigneur de Sillé41歲，母親Ermengarde de La Guerche de Pouancé31歲。她於1206年之前與Guillaume De la Guerche De Pouancé III結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1225年在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利享年35歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hersende de Sille- le- Guillaume于1190年出生在法国薩特希利艾勒-吉耀奧时，她的父亲Guillaume III de Sillé le Guillaume 5ème Seigneur de Sillé41岁，母亲Ermengarde de La Guerche de Pouancé31岁。她于1206年之前与Guillaume De la Guerche De Pouancé III结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1225年在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利享年35岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sille-
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of   Silk  .

variants.name: Silk
meanings: English: nickname for someone who made or sold silk, from Middle English silk, selk ‘silk’ (Old English seolc).
English: from a shortened form of the Middle English personal name Silkin, a diminutive of Sil (see Sill ).
Irish (Galway): in Ireland, adopted for Gaelic Ó Síoda, though never for Mac Síoda (see Sheedy ).

nameMeanings.name: le-
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Pierre, Jacques, Jean-Pierre, Marcel, Patrice, Yvon, Anite, Antoine, Edrice, Francois, Gesner, Ghislaine.
French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian: from the personal name Guillaume, French form of William .

variants.name: Guillet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Marc, Philippe, Pierre, Romain, Yvan.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Guillaume   ‘William’, derived from its short form   Guille   2, and perhaps also of   Guy   1. Compare   Deyette  ,   Diette  , and   Guillette  .
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Guillon
meanings: French: from a pet form of the personal name   Guillaume   ‘William’, derived from its short form   Guille   2.
Hispanic (Guillón): possibly of French origin (see above and Villon ).

variants.name: Guillou
meanings: French: from a pet form of the personal name   Guillaume   ‘William’, derived from its short form   Guille   2.

variants.name: Guillemette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Normand, Armand, Gilles, Jean-Paul, Leonie, Alcide, Anatole, Cecile, Emile, Fernand, Fernande.

Americanized form of French Guillemet: from a derivative of the personal name Guillaume . Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t. Compare Guilmette .
French: from Guillemette, a female personal name derived from Guillaume .

variants.name: Guillot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Francois, Landry, Serge, Amelie, Andree, Angelle, Antoine, Eugenie, Jacques, Jean Louis, Joffre.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Guillaume   ‘William’, derived from its short form   Guille   2. Compare   Diotte  ,   Guillotte  , and   Guyot  .

variants.name: William
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

en.lifeSummary: When Hersende de Sille- le- Guillaume was born in 1190, in Sillé-le-Guillaume, Sarthe, France, her father, Guillaume III de Sillé le Guillaume 5ème Seigneur de Sillé, was 41 and her mother, Ermengarde de La Guerche de Pouancé, was 31. She married Guillaume De la Guerche De Pouancé III before 1206. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1225, in Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France, at the age of 35.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hersende de Sille- le- Guillaume est née en 1190, à Sillé-le-Guillaume, Sarthe, France, son père, Guillaume III de Sillé le Guillaume 5ème Seigneur de Sillé, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Ermengarde de La Guerche de Pouancé, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Guillaume De la Guerche De Pouancé III avant 1206. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1225, à Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France, à l’âge de 35 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hersende de Sille- le- Guillaume 1190 in Sillé-le-Guillaume, Sarthe, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guillaume III de Sillé le Guillaume 5ème Seigneur de Sillé 41 und ihre Mutter Ermengarde de La Guerche de Pouancé 31. Sie hat vor 1206 Guillaume De la Guerche De Pouancé III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1225 im Alter von 35 Jahren in Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hersende de Sille- le- Guillaume è nata il nel 1190 nel Sillé-le-Guillaume, Sarthe, Francia, sua padre, Guillaume III de Sillé le Guillaume 5ème Seigneur de Sillé, aveva 41 anni e sua madre, Ermengarde de La Guerche de Pouancé, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Guillaume De la Guerche De Pouancé III prima del 1206. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1225, nel Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, all'età di 35 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hersende de Sille- le- Guillaumeが1190年にフランスサルスシルe-レ-グイラウメで生まれた時，父Guillaume III de Sillé le Guillaume 5ème Seigneur de Silléは41歳，母Ermengarde de La Guerche de Pouancéは31歳であった。1206年以前にGuillaume De la Guerche De Pouancé IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1225年にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメで35歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hersende de Sille- le- Guillaume(이)가 1190년 프랑스 사르테 시e-레-길로에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guillaume III de Sillé le Guillaume 5ème Seigneur de Sillé(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ermengarde de La Guerche de Pouancé(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1206년 이전 Guillaume De la Guerche De Pouancé III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1225년 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀에서 35세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hersende de Sille- le- Guillaume nasceu em 1190, em Sillé-le-Guillaume, Sarthe, França, seu pai, Guillaume III de Sillé le Guillaume 5ème Seigneur de Sillé, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Ermengarde de La Guerche de Pouancé, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Guillaume De la Guerche De Pouancé III antes 1206. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1225, em Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França, com 35 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hersende de Sille- le- Guillaume родилась в 1190 г. в местности силлe-ле-гуиллаюм, сарт, Франция, её отцу по имени Guillaume III de Sillé le Guillaume 5ème Seigneur de Sillé было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Ermengarde de La Guerche de Pouancé, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guillaume De la Guerche De Pouancé III перед 1206 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1225 г. в местности сине-маритим, норманди, Франция в возрасте 35 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hersende de Sille- le- Guillaume nació en 1190, en Sillé-le-Guillaume, Sarthe, Francia, su papá, Guillaume III de Sillé le Guillaume 5ème Seigneur de Sillé, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Ermengarde de La Guerche de Pouancé, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Guillaume De la Guerche De Pouancé III antes de 1206. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1225, en Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 35 años.
